sections.heading: Alert Conditions
sections.body:
When you configure alerts, you specify alert conditions and thresholds.
Review the possible [alert conditions](alert-conditions) for
which you can trigger alerts related to your clusters.

> **Note:** Free clusters and Flex clusters only trigger alerts related
> to the metrics supported by those clusters. For complete documentation
> on Free clusters and Flex clusters alert and metric limitations, see
> [M0 Cluster Limits](atlas-free-tier), and [Atlas Flex Limitations](flex-cluster-limitations).

sections.heading: Configure Alerts
sections.body:
To set which conditions trigger alerts and how users are notified,
[Configure Alert Settings](configure-alerts). You can configure alerts at the organization
or project level. Atlas provides default alerts at the project
level. You can clone existing alerts and configure maintenance window
alerts.

sections.heading: Resolve Alerts
sections.body:
When a condition triggers an alert, Atlas displays a warning symbol
on the cluster and sends alert notifications. Resolve these
alerts and work to prevent alert conditions from occurring in the
future. To learn how to fix the immediate problem, implement a
long-term solution, and monitor your progress, see
[Resolve Alerts](respond-to-alerts).

sections.heading: Alerts Workflow
sections.body:
When an [alert conditions](alert-conditions) is met, the following
alert lifecycle begins:

sections.heading: Step 1: The alert condition is met.
sections.body:
- For informational alerts like the [User has joined the organization](<User joined the organization>) alert, Atlas sends notifications as soon as
  the condition is met. These alerts don't appear in the
  Atlas UI.

  The informational alerts workflow ends here.

- Otherwise, Atlas retrieves metrics at regular intervals
  based on [Monitoring Data Storage Granularity](monitoring-storage-granularity).
  When the retrieved metrics indicate that an alert condition is
  met, Atlas tracks the alert condition until it ends, or
  until it remains past the amount of time specified in the
  **send if condition lasts at least** field.

  If the alert condition ends between sampling periods, Atlas
  doesn't track the alert condition or send any notifications.

> **Note:** Processing and network conditions may slightly delay alert
> notification delivery.

sections.heading: Step 2: The alert condition remains past the amount of time specified in the  field.
sections.body:
Atlas sends notifications. The alerts appear on the
**All Alerts** tab (organization alerts) or the
**Open Alerts** tab (project alerts).

sections.heading: Step 3: The alert remains active until it resolves, or you disable or delete it.
sections.body:
The following stages might occur:

- You [acknowledge the alert](acknowledge-alert). Atlas
  sends no further notifications until either the acknowledgement
  period ends, you resolve the alert condition, or you
  unacknowledge the alert. If an alert condition ends during an
  acknowledgment period, Atlas sends a notification.

- You [unacknowledge an alert](unacknowledge-alert) that
  you previously acknowledged. After you unacknowledge an active
  alert, Atlas resumes sending notifications at regular
  intervals until the condition resolves or you delete, disable,
  or re-acknowledge the alert.

- You [disable](disable-alert-setting). Atlas
  cancels active alerts related to the setting. A disabled alert
  setting remains visible but grayed-out and you can later
  re-enable it.

- You [enable an alert](enable-alert-setting) that you
  previously disabled.

- You [delete the Slack alert setting](delete-alert-setting). Atlas
  cancels active alerts related to the setting. A deleted alert
  setting does not remain visible.

- The alert condition resolves. The alert appears on the
  **All Alerts** tab (organization alerts) or the
  **Closed Alerts** tab (project alerts).

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.body: Before you get started with alerts, review the [Alert Basics](alert-basics).
